title: Rosa and the Singing Seeds
synopsis: Rosa discovers that her garden seeds won't grow and feels sad, but with help from her diverse group of friends who each contribute their unique skills and knowledge, she learns that friendship and working together can make beautiful things happen.

pages.text: Rosa loved her little garden. Every morning, she would peek outside her window and smile at the brown dirt where she planted her seeds. But today, Rosa felt sad. Her seeds had been in the ground for many days, but nothing was growing! "Why won't my flowers come up?" Rosa asked, touching the empty soil with her small hands.

illustration.description: Rosa, a young Latina girl with curly dark hair in two small ponytails, kneels sadly beside a small rectangular garden patch filled with dark brown soil. She's wearing a yellow t-shirt and denim overalls, with a disappointed expression as she gently touches the dirt. The garden is in a sunny backyard with a wooden fence, green grass, and her house visible in the background with warm morning light streaming down.
illustration.alt: Rosa looking sad while touching empty garden soil

pages.text: Rosa's friend Marcus came over to play. He saw Rosa looking sad by her garden. Marcus had beautiful dark skin and wore his favorite red shirt. "What's wrong, Rosa?" he asked kindly. Rosa pointed to her empty garden. "My seeds won't grow. I water them every day, but they just stay sleeping underground." Marcus thought carefully. "Maybe they need more than water," he said. "Let's ask my grandpa. He knows all about plants!"

illustration.description: Marcus, a young Black boy with short curly hair wearing a bright red shirt and blue shorts, stands beside Rosa near the garden. Rosa looks up at him hopefully while pointing to the soil. Marcus has a thoughtful, caring expression with his finger to his chin. The scene shows both children in the sunny backyard with fluffy white clouds in a blue sky and green grass around them.
illustration.alt: Marcus talking kindly to Rosa about her garden problem

pages.text: Soon Marcus came back with his grandpa and their friend Lily. Lily had long blonde hair and loved butterflies - she wore a purple shirt with a big butterfly on it. Grandpa smiled warmly at Rosa. "Let me see this garden," he said in his gentle voice. He knelt down and touched the soil. "Ah! Your seeds are fine, Rosa. But they need something special to grow big and strong." Rosa felt a little spark of hope. "What do they need?" she asked, feeling excited and curious.

illustration.description: Three people gather around Rosa's garden: Grandpa, an elderly Black man with gray hair and a kind smile wearing a green plaid shirt, kneels touching the soil; Lily, a young girl with long blonde hair in a purple butterfly t-shirt, stands nearby; Rosa and Marcus watch hopefully. The garden setting is bright and warm with dappled sunlight filtering through leaves above them, creating a cozy, hopeful atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Grandpa examining Rosa's garden while children watch hopefully

pages.text: "Seeds need friends to help them grow," Grandpa explained. "They need sunshine, which you have. They need water, which you give them. But they also need good soil and maybe some plant food." Lily clapped her hands. "I know where we can get good soil! My mom has a compost pile with the richest, darkest dirt." Marcus added, "And my family has plant food at home!" Rosa felt so happy. Her friends wanted to help her garden grow!

illustration.description: The group stands together in a circle around the garden, with Grandpa gesturing as he explains while the children listen attentively. Lily has her hands clasped together with excitement, Marcus is nodding eagerly, and Rosa's expression has changed from sad to happy and hopeful. The scene is bright and warm with golden sunlight, emphasizing the feeling of friendship and community.
illustration.alt: Grandpa explaining to excited children how to help plants grow

pages.text: All of Rosa's friends worked together. Lily brought a bucket of rich, dark soil that smelled like the forest. Marcus carried a small bag of plant food that would help the seeds grow strong. Rosa carefully mixed everything into her garden while Grandpa showed her how. Then she watered the soil gently. "Now we wait," said Grandpa with a twinkle in his eye. "But I think your seeds will be very happy now."

illustration.description: The children work together in the garden: Rosa mixes soil with a small hand trowel, Lily pours dark compost from a yellow bucket, and Marcus sprinkles plant food from a small bag. Grandpa supervises with a warm smile. Everyone looks busy and happy, working as a team. The garden now looks richer and darker, and there's a watering can nearby. The scene radiates cooperation and friendship.
illustration.alt: Friends working together to improve Rosa's garden soil

pages.text: A few days later, Rosa woke up and looked out her window. She gasped with joy! Tiny green shoots were poking up from the soil. "They're growing! They're growing!" she called to her friends. Marcus, Lily, and Grandpa came running to see. Rosa felt so happy and grateful. "Thank you for helping my seeds," she said, hugging each of her friends. "Now I know that everything grows better with good friends to help." The little green plants danced in the morning breeze, as if they were happy too.

illustration.description: Rosa jumps with joy beside her garden, which now has several small green sprouts emerging from the rich dark soil. Her three friends stand around her, all smiling and celebrating. Rosa has her arms outstretched in happiness, Marcus is clapping, Lily is pointing excitedly at the sprouts, and Grandpa has a proud, satisfied smile. The morning sun creates a golden glow over the scene, and the tiny green plants look vibrant and healthy.
illustration.alt: Rosa and friends celebrating the sprouting plants in the garden

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Rosa feel when her seeds wouldn't grow?
questions.explanation: Rosa felt sad because her seeds wouldn't grow even though she watered them every day.

questions.question: Who helped Rosa figure out what her seeds needed?
questions.explanation: Marcus brought his grandpa, who knew all about plants and could help Rosa's garden.

questions.question: What did Lily bring to help the garden?
questions.explanation: Lily brought rich, dark soil from her mom's compost pile to help Rosa's seeds grow.

questions.question: How did Rosa feel when her plants finally started growing?
questions.explanation: Rosa felt happy and grateful when she saw the green shoots growing and thanked her friends for helping.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Help complete Rosa's story by choosing the right words!
pages.text: Rosa felt ___ when her seeds wouldn't grow. Her friend Marcus wanted to ___. They brought good ___ and plant food to the garden.

pages.title: More Missing Words
pages.instructions: Choose the words that make Rosa's story complete!
pages.text: All the ___ worked together to help Rosa's garden. When the plants started growing, Rosa felt very ___.
